Home/Fire pit builder northern virginia

Fire Pits — The Heart of Your Outdoor Living Space

Custom gas & wood-burning fire pits — standalone, built-in, and deck-integrated designs

Call (571) 655-7207Get Free Estimate

Fire Pit Options & Pricing

TypePrice RangeFuelBest For
Portable fire bowl/table$500–$2,000Propane/gasFlexibility, rentals, small spaces
Built-in gas fire pit$3,000–$8,000Natural gas lineClean, instant, deck-adjacent
Stone surround (wood-burning)$5,000–$12,000Wood logsLarge lots, campfire experience
Fire pit + seating wall$8,000–$18,000Gas or woodGathering spot, entertaining
Fire pit + paver patio combo$12,000–$25,000Gas or woodComplete outdoor room at grade
Fire table (rectangular)$2,000–$6,000Propane/natural gasDining integration, modern look
Deck-integrated gas feature$4,000–$10,000Natural gasBuilt into deck with non-combustible pad

Gas vs Wood-Burning Fire Pit

Gas Fire Pit (Recommended for NoVA)

  • Instant on/off — no startup or cleanup
  • No smoke, no sparks, no ash
  • Adjustable flame height
  • Safe near deck/house structures
  • HOA-preferred (most allow gas, restrict wood)
  • No wood storage or hauling
  • Cost: $3k–$10k + gas line ($1k–$3k if new)

Wood-Burning Fire Pit

  • Authentic campfire experience (crackle, smell)
  • No gas line required — lower install cost
  • Higher heat output
  • Requires 15+ ft from structures (fire code)
  • Smoke can bother neighbors on small lots
  • Some HOAs prohibit wood-burning
  • Cost: $5k–$12k (no gas line needed)

Fire Pit + Deck Integration

Can I put a fire pit on my composite deck? Not directly — but we have safe solutions:

  • Non-combustible pad: We build a stone/tile insert into the deck surface (2x2 to 3x3 ft) where the gas fire pit sits. Protects the composite from heat.
  • Fire table with glass guard: Rectangular gas fire tables produce minimal radiant heat — safe on composite with a heat shield underneath.
  • Adjacent ground-level: Fire pit on a paver/stone pad at the base of deck stairs. Most popular — separates the fire zone from the deck surface.
  • Multi-level design: Upper deck for dining, lower stone patio with built-in fire pit. See multi-level deck options.

Real Fire Pit Projects

$6,500 — Built-in Gas Fire Pit, Ashburn

Round stone-surround gas fire pit on existing paver patio. Natural stone cap, fire glass media, gas line run from house. Broadlands HOA approved.

$14,000 — Fire Pit + Seating Wall, Leesburg

Custom stone fire pit with 180-degree seating wall (seats 8). Flagstone cap. Gas-powered. Integrated into existing landscape. Loudoun County permit for gas line.

$22,000 — Fire Pit + Paver Patio, Centreville

250 sqft paver patio with centered fire pit and surrounding Adirondack seating area. Gas line, landscape lighting, and planting beds. Complete backyard transformation.

FAQ

Fire pit cost in NoVA?

Gas built-in: $3k-$8k. Stone surround: $5k-$12k. Fire pit + patio combo: $12k-$25k. Gas line adds $1k-$3k if new.

Can I put one on my deck?

Not directly on composite. Options: non-combustible pad insert, fire table with heat shield, or adjacent ground-level patio.

Gas vs wood?

Gas: clean, instant, safe near structures, HOA-friendly. Wood: authentic, no gas line needed. Most NoVA HOAs prefer gas.
